page: Vultr vcg-a16-96c-878g-256vram is a Dedicated Metal GPU server instance designed for highly parallelized workloads. The system is powered by an AMD x86_64 processor configured with 96 physical cores and 96 vCPUs, operating with a single thread per core. It is equipped with 877.0 GB of system memory, which translates to a resource density of approximately 9.14 GB of RAM per core.

The primary accelerator component consists of 16 NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs, providing a combined total of 256 GB of VRAM. For local data storage, the server is bundled with a 3072 GB NVMe SSD. The network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

This hardware profile delivers high resource density by combining a large physical core count with sixteen discrete graphics processors. This configuration is optimized for specialized workloads such as high-density virtual desktop infrastructure (VDI), video streaming, media transcoding, and parallelized graphics rendering.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-96c-878g-256vram is a Dedicated Metal GPU server configured with an AMD x86_64 processor featuring 96 physical cores and 96 vCPUs. The system is provisioned with 877.0 GB of system memory, yielding 9.14 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, the instance integrates 16 NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs with a combined 256 GB of VRAM. Local storage is supported by a 3072 GB NVMe SSD, and the network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. This high-density GPU and memory profile is designed for parallel processing tasks, rendering, video streaming, and virtual desktop infrastructure (VDI) environments.

tagline: A high-density GPU server featuring sixteen graphics accelerators, ninety-six physical cores, and substantial system memory for parallel processing workloads.
